mnemonics.title: Four Deadlock Conditions
mnemonics.content: M.H.N.C - MUTUAL EXCLUSION, HOLD AND WAIT, NO PREEMPTION, CIRCULAR WAIT. Remember as 'MOST HARMless Nations Collude' (Mutual, Hold, No Preemption, Circular)

mnemonics.title: Banker's Algorithm Steps
mnemonics.content: SAFE SEQ: WORK=AVAILABLE, FINISH=FALSE. LOOP: FIND process where NEED≤WORK, if found add ALLOCATION to WORK and mark FINISH. REPEAT until all FINISH or deadlock. This is S-A-F-E: Start, Allocate, Finish, Evaluate

acronyms.usage: Visual representation of resource allocation to detect potential deadlocks
Simplified graph showing which processes wait for others - cycle means deadlock

acronyms.expansion: Mutual Exclusion - Hold and Wait - No Preemption - Circular Wait
acronyms.usage: Four necessary conditions that MUST ALL hold for deadlock to occur

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a circular table where each person is holding a fork (resource) and waiting for the fork held by the person to their right. No one can eat because everyone is waiting.
Think of a classroom where students (processes) need books (resources). The teacher (OS) checks if there are enough books for all students to complete their work before letting anyone borrow.
Use circles for processes (P1, P2) and squares for resources (R1, R2). Draw arrows from process to resource for request, from resource to process for assignment. A cycle means potential deadlock.
Think of a bank manager who only lends money if they can ensure all loans can be repaid even in worst case. They keep 'available' cash and check 'need' before approving any request.

analogies.analogy: Four cars at a four-way intersection, each waiting for the other to move. Each holds the 'right of way' (resource) and waits for another. No one can proceed - classic deadlock.
A bank manager who lends money only if convinced all borrowers can repay. They maintain a 'safe buffer' and check each loan request against maximum needs before approval.
A teacher taking away a toy from one fighting child and giving it to another - preemption may cause upset (data inconsistency) but resolves the conflict.
Ignoring a slow leak in your roof because fixing it is expensive and leaks are rare. You hope it won't cause major damage - like pretending deadlocks won't happen.
